title: Sources: Cardinals to cut QB Murray next week
content: The Cardinals informed quarterback Kyler Murray that they intend to release him on the first day of the league year next Wednesday, barring a trade between now and then, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter on Monday.

title: Prosecutors to offer plea agreements in poker case

title: Colts place rarely used transition tag on QB Jones
content: The Colts place the rarely used transition tag on QB Daniel Jones, preserving their right to match any offer sheet in free agency.

title: Plum, Stewart question PA's handling of CBA talks

title: World Cup playoff tickets on sale for less than $17
content: Tickets for the playoffs in Mexico that will determine two of the final World Cup berths are being sold by FIFA for less than $17.

title: Team USA lauds growing chemistry in WBC tuneup
content: Team USA manager Mark DeRosa said the team had a "different feel" to the 2023 squad, citing how everybody stayed in the dugout through the end of their 15-1 exhibition win over the Giants.

title: Sources: Bears center Dalman retiring at age 27
content: Pro Bowl center Drew Dalman has informed the Bears that he's retiring at age 27 after four seasons with the Atlanta Falcons and one with Chicago, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Iowa St. discontinues women's gymnastics team
content: Iowa State is ending its women's gymnastics program. The school's AD called the move "the right decision for our athletics program and our student athletes," citing a pattern of issues within the program.

title: "Everything's Knocked Out, 3rd Wave Coming": Trump's Fresh Warning For Iran
content: US President Donald Trump warned on Tuesday that the US is initiating a "third wave" of military strikes against Iran. This follows the elimination of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, during the initial phases of Operation Epic Fury.

title: Trump Warns Of Ending "All Trade" With Spain For Not Backing Attack On Iran
content: US President Donald Trump on Tuesday lashed out at Britain and Spain for not fully backing his attack on Iran, as he threatened to end "all trade" with Spain.

title: "Could Take Months": Former US Security Adviser's Iran War Prediction
content: "I terms of the operation itself, I think four to five weeks is a minimum. I think this could take months," John Bolton said.

title: US Took "Stupid Decision" To Attack While In Negotiations: Iran's UN Envoy
content: The United States made a "totally stupid decision" to attack Iran while in negotiations, and betrayed Gulf nations by trashing their diplomatic efforts, Tehran's UN ambassador said Tuesday.

title: RTE Quota Students Made To Do Menial Work, High Court Seeks Explanation
content: The court has now directed the School Education Secretary to file a detailed affidavit by March 11, explaining how children meant to be protected under the RTE Act allegedly ended up handling cement, sand and paint brushes instead of textbooks.

title: Middle East Tensions Cast Shadow Over Assam's Orthodox Tea Exports
content: Amid rising geopolitical tensions in Iran and the broader Middle East region, concerns are mounting within the Indian tea industry, particularly for Assam's orthodox tea segment.

title: Pentagon Warns Of Low Missile Stock If Iran War Rages For 10 More Days: Report
content: As the conflict between the US, Israel and Iran continues to rage on for the fourth day today, leaks from the Pentagon have suggested that if the US continues to strike Iran for 10 more days, the stocks of critical missiles could run low.

title: Rs 1,88,70,00,00,00,000: The Projected Cost Of Trump's War In Iran
content: The figure has been estimated by Kent Smetters, director of the Penn Wharton Budget Model and one of the US' foremost fiscal analysts.

title: BJP Names Ahom And Tea Garden Leaders As Candidates For Rajya Sabha Polls
content: The BJP has named minister Jogen Mohan and MLA Terash Gowalla as candidates for the Rajya Sabha election from Assam. The other Rajya Sabha seat is likely to go to the BJP's ally UPPL.

title: Schools Shut In Kashmir Till March 7 Amid Protest Over Ayatollah Khamenei's Killing
content: Amid protests and ongoing security restrictions, the Jammu and Kashmir government has announced closure of schools and colleges till the end of this week.

title: Loud Explosions Heard Across Dubai, Doha As Iran Continues Retaliation
content: Loud explosions were heard tonight across Dubai and Doha as Iran continued its drone and missile attacks on nations in the Middle East where the US military has a presence.

title: Iraq Halts Production At Major Oil Field After Strait Of Hormuz Closure
content: Iraq reduced production at a major oil field on Tuesday after the Strait of Hormuz was effectively closed, according to an official document and oil ministry sources.

title: Video: Passengers Flung Into Air, 2 Dead As Pickup Truck Hits Divider
content: A speeding pickup truck lost control and collided with a divider on the Agra-Lucknow Expressway in Uttar Pradesh's Unnao district on Tuesday.

title: Parked Passenger Aircraft Destroyed In US-Israeli Attack At Bushehr Airport: Report
content: An Iranian aircraft was destroyed on the ground at Bushehr aiport during a series of military strikes. The incident was reported in Iranian media on Tuesday.

title: US Stocks Tumble, Join Global Sell-Off As Iran War Shakes Up US Markets
content: Wall Street stocks tumbled early Tuesday, joining a global sell-off as markets worry about a long-running Middle East war boosting oil prices and inflation.

title: Khamenei Died During Treatment Day After Strike, Claims Aide
content: "At the time of attack, Ayatollah Khamenei's son-in-law and daughter died on the spot. However, Ayatollah Khamenei was not martyred. He was taken to the hospital, and he passed away during treatment the next day," said Abdul Majid Hakim Elah

title: Strait Of Hormuz: When A Narrow Waterway Dictates India's Economic Weather
content: The crisis unfolding across the Middle East has pushed an old geographic truth back into the foreground: a thin blue channel, wedged between hostile shores, can rattle an entire economy thousands of kilometres away.

title: On India’s fighter jet acquisitions
content: The procurement of the Rafale jets is not just fleet expansion. It is a structural shift in how India positions itself within global defence hierarchies. Securing access to mission software and autonomy to integrate weapons in fighter aircraft is central to advancing India’s efforts to indigenise defence development.

title: Finland President Alexander Stubb is in India from March 4 to 7
content: The visit, featuring a high-level delegation of Ministers, officials, and business leaders, aims to strengthen bilateral ties and expand cooperation between India and Finland across various sectors

title: This Assam startup makes sustainable paper from water hyacinth
content: Wildlife enthusiasts Aniket Dhar and Rupankar Bhattacharjee turn an invasive aquatic weed into chemical-free paper while supporting local livelihoods

content: Assam Chief Minister inaugurated the park on a day a Sikkim specialist announced a hybrid orchid named after the prime rhino habitat

title: Bihar CM Nitish Kumar’s son likely to join active politics soon: JD(U) leaders
content: Posters of Nishant Kumar demanding him to join active politics too had earlier adorned walls of the JD-U state headquarters in Patna

title: Trinamool cries foul over tea garden workers struck off  electoral rolls or kept in adjudication list
content: In January, the ECI allowed records of Tea Gardens and Cinchona plantations as documents for SIR 2026

content: Mr. Masood described the death of the Iranian leader Khamenei as a “sad event” and personally conveyed his sympathies during the meeting

title: Tamil Nadu’s saga of controversial Governors begins with Sri Prakasa of the 1950s

content: Junaid Azim Mattu said in a Kashmir today fellow mainstream leaders have “surrendered and sold their tongues to the BJP-led Government and chosen to sound shamefully diplomatic over the assassination of Khamenei and the brutal war on Iran and Palestine”.

content: He adds that actor’s Vijay’s outfit still operated like a “fan club”  and doesn’t have “a command and control structure”

content: In a communication dated March 2, he wrote to the T.N. Chief Minister expressing Karnataka’s strong support on the issue

title: Indian airlines plan to operate 58 flights to West Asia on March 4: Govt
content: "Indian carriers are further planning 58 flights on 4th March, including 30 by IndiGo and 23 by Air India and Air India Express," Civil Aviation Ministry said.

title: Siddaramaiah rubbishes allegations of spying on D.K. Shivakumar loyalists
content: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah rubbished allegations of spying and phone tapping made by Leader of Opposition in the Assembly R. Ashok and JD(S) leader and Union Minister for Heavy Industries and Steel H.D. Kumaraswamy

title: Under pressure from the Dalit Left, recruitment GO likely to come up for discussion again in Karnataka Cabinet
content: Dalit left community delegation, including Ministers K.H. Muniyappa and R.B. Thimmapur, met Chief Minister Siddaramaiah on Tuesday and is learnt to have exerted pressure for withdrawal of GO on recruitment

title: D.K. Shivakumar urges Karnataka MPs to put pressure on Centre to seek approval to raise Almatti dam height
content: Andhra Pradesh government has written to the Centre not to approve the proposal to raise the dam height to 524.26 metres

title: Canada PM's visit to India built strong foundation for truly renewed partnership: Modi
content: The Canadian Prime Minister was in India from February 27 to March 2, during which India and Canada sealed key pacts on supplies of Uranium and critical minerals and agreed to conclude a comprehensive economic partnership agreement soon.

title: Karnataka HC directs State government to formulate policy for accurate content management on govt. websites
content: Justice Suraj Govindaraj has issued the directions while dismissing a petition filed in 2016 by Jain International Residential School raising certain disputes over correctness of levy of water charges

content: ‘Courts need to remain vigilant against any temptation to expand the scope of review beyond the narrow boundaries prescribed by the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code,’ the judgment says

content: Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu directs APNRTS to ensure uninterrupted food supply and all possible support to expatriates facing hardship, says APNRTS president Ravi Kumar Vemuru

content: She has already served in the administration department at the Central Jail in Kot Balwal, Jammu, and the Kupwara Jail.

content: Sources said the DMK raised its offer from 25 to 27 Assembly seats and one Rajya Sabha seat, while the Congress leadership is demanding 30 Assembly seats and a Rajya Sabha seat.

content: Twenty-four youths from Meitei, Kuki, Naga and Pangal (Muslim) communities to travel across five cities in first such initiative since 2023 ethnic violence

title: BJP leader Ravi Shankar Prasad to head Lok Sabha’s Privileges Committee
content: The panel examines cases referred by the Speaker and submits reports on matters where breaches of privilege are alleged

content: When the victims were passing by motor cycle, they were caught hold of and asked about stolen goats, police  said; the two victims were then then thrashed with sticks

content: Deputy CM Bhatti Vikramarka compliments engineers, officials and staff of power utilities for handling the unrestricted power demand

content: Organisations should go in for company-approved apps and relevant security tools to address internal threats, says expert

content: Although the cleanup drive started early in the afternoon, waste was not removed from several parts including the bypass area near Chackai, Fort and Vazhuthacaud even at night, unlike the previous years

content: The nomination of Minister Jogen Mohan and MLA Terash Gowala is significant as they are from communities demanding Scheduled Tribe status

content: Following his expulsion from the Biju Janata Dal in 2002, the former Union Coal Minister had pulled off a surprise win in the election to the Upper House as an independent candidate

content: The agreement signed by Mr. Stalin and Mr. Sudheesh said the seat-sharing committee would decide on the number of Assembly seats to be allotted to the party.

content: On Tuesday, the penultimate day of the 10-day Attukal Pongala festival, the feverish excitement that had gripped the city for days reached a crescendo with thousands of women offering ‘pongala’

title: Judicial independence extends to freedom of a judge to dissent or diverge from colleagues, says J. Nagarathna
content: ‘Judicial independence is not exhausted by insulation from the political branches. It also requires that each judge be free to form and express his or her own considered view of the law, even when that view diverges from colleagues’

title: Verdict on bail application of former Travancore Devaswom Board president expected on March 4
content: Having already secured statutory bail last month in a related case involving the theft of gold from the Sabarimala Ayyapa temple’s doorframe, a favorable ruling on Wednesday will pave the way for Padmakumar’s release from prison

title: Holi celebrated with joy in Belagavi
content: The police restricted traffic in the Old City areas like Pangul Galli, Risaldar Galli, Khadak Galli, etc.

title: Seven held for murdering two persons in indiscriminate attack in Nanguneri
content: Two among those arrested have also been named as accused in the attempt to murder Chinnadurai, a Schedule Caste boy, on August 9, 2023

title: PIL seeks removal of ‘Kerala’ from the title of ‘The Kerala Story 2 – Goes Beyond’
content: Petitioners seeks a High Court directive to the Centre and to the CBFC to permit screening of the film with a disclaimer that it was based on a purely fictional theme

title: Garbage woes, building violations take centre stage at east corporation meeting
content: Mr. Rao directs officials to make it mandatory for buildings with more than two floors to place two large dustbins for dry and wet waste

title: Ayatollah’s son Mojtaba Khamenei emerges as front-runner to succeed slain Supreme Leader
content: Iran's Assembly of Experts is reportedly deliberating on Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the Supreme Leader, as his successor, with the IRGC pushing for his appointment. While Mojtaba is the front-runner, some clerics express reservations due to potential targeting by the US and Israel. The selection process is ongoing amid heightened tensions.

title: Bullets rain in Haryana: Man pumps 8 shots into wife outside home
content: A man with a criminal record, facing three cases including murder, allegedly shot his wife dead outside their home in Jhajjar, Haryana. Police suspect a domestic dispute led to the fatal shooting of eight bullets. The accused, Sanjay, fled the scene and remains at large despite extensive manhunt operations. Investigations are ongoing, with authorities reviewing CCTV footage and conducting raids.

title: 'Not happy with UK either': Trump slams European nations, singles out Spain over Iran campaign
content: US President Trump has sharply criticized European nations, particularly Spain, for their stance on the US-Israel military campaign against Iran. While Germany is praised, Spain's refusal to allow base usage for operations against Iran has drawn Trump's ire. The conflict escalates with regional attacks and rising oil prices.

title: Happy Holi 2026: 50+ wishes, messages, quotes, images, & more to share with your loved ones
content: Holi 2026 is here, falling on Wednesday, March 4th, promising a mid-week celebration of colors. The article provides a comprehensive guide to Holi wishes, messages, quotes, and status updates tailored for family, friends, and colleagues, ensuring everyone can share the festive spirit.

title: Tech employees across America send open letter to Pentagon on Anthropic
content: Hundreds of tech employees have signed an open letter urging the Department of War and Congress to withdraw Anthropic's "supply chain risk" designation. This action followed a failed contract negotiation, leading to a presidential directive to cease using Anthropic's technology. The letter argues this precedent punishes American innovation and urges a resolution through commercial channels.

title: ‘Planned to travel 31 countries’: Canadian Sikh on world bike tour dies in crash in Peru
content: A 42-year-old Punjab native, Raghbir Singh Bharowal, residing in Surrey, Canada, has died in a motorcycle accident in Peru. Bharowal, a father of three and a Canadian immigration consultant, was on a solo 'Flying Khalsa World Tour' when he met with the fatal accident. His family lost contact with him three days prior to learning of his death.

title: World Cup exit sparks row in Pak cricket: Amir blasts PCB over fine on players
content: Mohammad Amir has criticized the Pakistan Cricket Board's reported PKR 50 lakh fine on players after their T20 World Cup exit. He argued that selectors and administrators should also be held accountable for poor team selections and performance, suggesting dropping underperforming players instead of fining them.

title: For families, reunions add colour to festivities as stranded people return safely
content: Families in Delhi are celebrating Holi and Eid with renewed joy after reuniting with relatives who returned from West Asia amid escalating tensions. Passengers arriving at IGI Airport expressed relief at being back in India, emphasizing the safety of their homeland after experiencing a tense but manageable situation abroad.

title: Netflix CEO: WB deal will force Paramount CEO David Ellison to layoff thousands
content: Netflix's Ted Sarandos revealed the company bowed out of the Warner Bros. Discovery bidding war, deeming Paramount's debt-laden offer too risky. He warned of significant layoffs and reduced production at the rival studio. Despite the loss, Netflix will invest its saved funds into its core business and expand its theatrical presence.

title: Watch: Flames, black smoke rise near US Consulate in Dubai after drone strike
content: Dubai authorities extinguished a fire near the US consulate following a drone strike. Emergency teams responded swiftly, and no injuries were reported. The UAE's air defense systems intercepted numerous missiles and drones. The nation maintains its right to self-defense amidst ongoing regional tensions. The UAE reiterates its neutral stance in the conflict.

title: Karnataka touches 40°C: Heatwave risk across state, UV radiation levels spike in Bengaluru
content: Bengaluru and Karnataka are facing unusually harsh early summer conditions with soaring temperatures and extreme UV radiation levels. Weather experts warn of significant health risks, including sunburn, eye damage, and heat-related illnesses, especially for vulnerable groups. Precautions like protective clothing and avoiding peak sun hours are strongly advised.

title: 'Has become star in short time': Ex cricketer blasts Abhishek before semifinal
content: Abhishek Sharma's batting form is a concern for India's T20 World Cup 2026 semifinal clash against England. Former cricketers Manoj Tiwary and Irfan Pathan have pointed out issues with his approach and technique. Sharma has struggled for consistency in the tournament. He needs to improve his performance to help India win matches.

title: Sam Altman says he can't speak for the Pentagon, but Anthropic seemed more focused on controls
content: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman suggested Anthropic's stricter contractual demands may have led to their failed Pentagon deal. While OpenAI secured a contract, Anthropic was declared a national security risk. Altman emphasized OpenAI's reliance on technical safeguards over contract language for safety in government deployments.

title: Wishing you a warm Holi! Mercury may hit 34°C in Delhi today
content: Delhi is set for a significant warm-up, with temperatures expected to climb from 34°C on Holi to 36°C by March 8. Clear skies and prolonged sunshine are driving this rise, as the city experiences a lack of western disturbances. Expect daytime temperatures to remain appreciably above normal for the next week.

title: Ayatollah Khamenei to be buried in his hometown, Mashhad
content: Iran's former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in a joint US-Israel strike, with his burial planned for Mashhad. The assassination marks a significant escalation, with US President Trump calling it justice and Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu noting signs of his demise.

title: Pahalgam attack: GoPro trail leads to Chinese city; court allows NIA to seek Beijing help
content: NIA has requested China's assistance to identify the buyer of a GoPro camera, crucial evidence in a Lashkar-e-Taiba attack. Activated in Dongguan, China, the camera's origin is vital for tracing terrorist reconnaissance and operational links. This move highlights international cooperation efforts in combating terror.

title: 6 returning home for Holi dead as bus rams van on Yamuna Expressway; 7 injured
content: A speeding sleeper bus collided with a van carrying 13 passengers on the Yamuna Expressway, resulting in six fatalities, including two couples. The victims were en route to Rajasthan to celebrate Holi. Police have arrested the bus driver and conductor, with legal proceedings ongoing.

title: Middle East tensions: Have critical stakes in Gulf, will act in national interest, says govt
content: India expresses deep anxiety over the escalating West Asia conflict, highlighting the critical stakes for nearly one crore Indian nationals in the Gulf and the potential economic repercussions. The Ministry of External Affairs strongly condemned attacks on merchant shipping, noting the loss of Indian lives and emphasizing the need for restraint and dialogue.

title: UK: Hindu temple thrown lifeline by judge after council plans to sell its premises to a mosque
content: A Hindu temple in Peterborough secured a High Court injunction halting the sale of its building to a mosque. The Bharat Hindu Samaj Temple, serving 18,000 devotees and offering community services, challenged the council's decision after being outbid. The judge found a strong case for interim relief, preventing irreversible steps.

title: Rashmika Mandanna's sindoor and mangalsutra grab attention post-wedding to Vijay Deverakonda
content: Newlyweds Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda are radiating post-wedding bliss, recently spotted in traditional attire after their Udaipur nuptials. The couple has been actively participating in post-wedding rituals, including a housewarming ceremony and a visit to the Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams temple. Their grand Hyderabad reception is scheduled for March 4, promising more celebratory moments.

title: AI summit protest: Youth Congress president Uday Bhanu & others released from Tihar jail
content: Indian Youth Congress president Uday Bhanu Chib has been released from Tihar jail after being granted bail. He asserted that the Congress and its youth wing will continue their opposition to the India-US deal, claiming it harms farmers. Chib was arrested for his alleged role in a protest at Bharat Mandapam.

title: Dhurandhar star Ayesha Khan reveals she was once the victim of an attempted rape
content: Ayesha Khan, who has been basking in the success of Shararat song from Dhurandhar, has candidly addressed the dark side of being a woman in the public eye — from daily online sexualisation to actual threats of sexual violence and an attempted rape in her life.

title: West Asia crisis: 38 Indian ships stuck in Persian Gulf; 3 sailors dead
content: Thirty-eight Indian-flagged ships, carrying crude and LNG with nearly 1,100 seafarers, are stranded in the Persian Gulf due to the West Asia conflict. Three Indian sailors died and one was injured on foreign-flagged vessels in recent attacks off Oman. Shipping authorities are prioritizing seafarer safety and maritime asset security.

title: Russia says India hints ‘renewed interest’ in more crude imports amid Iran crisis
content: Russia reports India's renewed interest in larger crude oil imports amid Strait of Hormuz disruptions. Moscow sees this as a chance to reduce discounts to Asian buyers. India, however, assures sufficient reserves and diversified sourcing, with only 40% of its crude imports passing through the critical waterway.

title: Railways unlikely to seek extra loan from JICA to meet higher cost of Bullet train project
content: Indian Railways will absorb an additional Rs 90,000 crore for the Ahmedabad-Mumbai Bullet Train, bringing the total cost to Rs 1.98 lakh crore.  No further loans from JICA are planned. The ministry seeks Cabinet approval for revised funding, exploring options like finance ministry support and multilateral financing for future corridors.

title: MoD inks Rs 2,182 crore deal with Russia for ship missiles
content: India's defence ministry has boosted naval and coast guard capabilities with two major deals worth over Rs 5,000 crore. The Indian Coast Guard will receive six advanced maritime helicopters from HAL, enhancing its security roles. Meanwhile, the Indian Navy is acquiring advanced Shtil surface-to-air missiles from Russia to bolster warship defence against aerial threats.

title: Parliament must debate 'dissonance' in foreign policy: Congress MP Sonia Gandhi
content: Congress leaders Sonia and Rahul Gandhi criticized PM Modi's government for its "silence" on the attack on Iran, calling it a "tacit endorsement." They questioned India's foreign policy direction and its standing in the world, especially concerning the "Global South," urging a return to India's core values of sovereignty and peace.

title: Iran crisis: PM Modi talks to leaders of Qatar, Oman, Kuwait
content: Prime Minister Modi engaged with leaders of Qatar, Oman, and Kuwait, condemning Iranian attacks and reaffirming India's commitment to regional peace. These discussions, completing talks with all GCC nations, highlighted India's focus on protecting its diaspora and ensuring stability. Modi stressed dialogue and diplomacy for restoring harmony and thanked leaders for their support to the Indian community.

title: 'Oil will reach $200/barrel': Iran threatens to target 'all economic centres' in Gulf
content: Iran has warned of retaliation against "all economic centres" in the Middle East if its "main centres" are targeted by US-Israeli attacks. The IRGC general stated the Strait of Hormuz has been closed, predicting oil prices will surge. This escalation follows joint US-Israel operations and Iranian strikes on regional economic hubs and facilities.

title: PM Modi calls for export push, puts biopharma at centre stage
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has signaled a strategic shift in India's export policy, moving from cost competitiveness to quality-driven growth. He urged industries to scale up research, adopt global standards, and align with emerging supply chains to become a trusted manufacturing partner in a reshaped global trade landscape.

title: After 'solving' 8 wars, Trump gives 10 interviews to explain initiating 9th
content: President Trump offered multiple justifications for the air campaign against Iran, including a preemptive strike against its supreme leader. He cited intelligence of assassination plots, election interference, and an imminent nuclear threat, though evidence remains unrevealed. Trump's strategy aims to control the narrative, presenting varied reasons to different outlets.

title: US, Israel attacks Iran: Scheduled, charter flights to be used for repatriation
content: India is actively working to repatriate thousands of its citizens stranded in West Asian countries like the UAE and Qatar. The government is utilizing a combination of charter and scheduled flights, with airlines warned against fare hikes. This effort follows a high-level review of the West Asia crisis.

title: US closes 3 Gulf embassies, urges Americans to leave Middle East; what's Trump planning?
content: US embassies in Saudi Arabia and Kuwait have closed following drone attacks, with Americans urged to leave 14 Middle Eastern nations. President Trump signaled intensified military action against Iran, not ruling out ground troops. The conflict has widened, impacting oil supplies and causing market turmoil, with significant casualties reported.

title: Apple launches MacBook Pro With M5 Pro and M5 Max chips: Up to 4x faster AI, starts at Rs 2,49,900 in India
content: Apple's new MacBook Pro with M5 Pro and M5 Max chips delivers up to 4x faster AI performance over the previous generation, with Neural Accelerators baked into every GPU core. Storage starts at 1TB for M5 Pro and 2TB for M5 Max, with SSD speeds doubling to 14.5GB/s. India pricing begins at Rs 2,49,900, with pre-orders opening March 4 and availability from March 11.

title: 'Thursday night is his night': Will Jos Buttler boss India at Wankhede in semifinal?
content: Buttler had a difficult ODI World Cup in India in 2023 and has not made a significant impact so far in the ongoing T20 World Cup. This comes in a country where he has scored heavily in the IPL, including the 2025 season.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' dominates over 'Toxic' in North America
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' is outpacing Yash's 'Toxic' in North American pre-sales, with advance bookings exceeding $100K USD. While 'Toxic' leads domestic buzz and screen count, especially in the South, 'Dhurandhar 2' shows strong momentum for its Hindi premiere. Both films are set for a major box office clash.

title: Iran burns, China waits: Why Beijing won’t fight for Tehran
content: Despite Iran's critical economic reliance on China, Beijing's response to strikes on its leader was limited to verbal condemnation and calls for de-escalation.  China prioritizes its broader economic interests and balanced regional ties over defending Tehran militarily, demonstrating a strategic calculus that favors diplomacy and deniability over direct intervention or security guarantees.

title: Middle East on boil: Russia says ready to help India with energy supplies in case of disruption
content: Russia has offered energy supply support to India amidst Middle East tensions. New Delhi, while monitoring the escalating Gulf crisis, assures sufficient crude oil and petroleum product reserves. Diversified sourcing and strategic stockpiles provide a buffer, with a 24/7 control room ensuring national energy security and protecting citizens in the region.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for March 4
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for March 4, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: In a historic first, Indian women's cricket team nominated for Laureus honour
content: India’s women’s cricket team has been nominated for the 2026 Laureus World Team of the Year Award after winning their maiden World Cup title. They are the first women’s cricket team to earn this honour, highlighted by a record 339-run chase against Australia. The winners will be announced in Madrid on April 20 by the Laureus World Sports Academy jury.

title: Wedding date buzz: Adnaan Shaikh confirms Mr Faisu's wedding plans; fans await July update
content: Social media sensation Mr. Faisu, also known as Faisal Shaikh, is in the spotlight once again as whispers of his impending wedding grow louder, with a celebration expected this July. His brother, Adnaan Shaikh, has happily confirmed that the marriage will take place this year, although the exact date is still under wraps.

title: Way-too-early college football coaching hot seat tiers
content: college football's coaching carousel moves fast and furiously, claiming coaches from the expected (Oklahoma State's Mike Gundy, Florida's Billy Napier) to the downright shocking (Michigan's Sherrone Moore). the SEC and Big Ten had a combined nine coaching changes, including at four programs -- Michigan, LSU, Auburn and Florida .

title: Yamal is eclipsing young Ronaldo, Messi. Are Barça too dependent on him already?
content: lamine Yamal has 100 combined goals and assists for club and country as a teenager . the two great modern players, Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo, both had only five and four goal contributions . Yamal is 60 ahead of where real madrid and france forward Kylian Mbappé was at the same age .

content: the six league champions will earn automatic berths into the 16-team NCAA field . the other 10 spots going to the top teams in the NPI rankings . regional play in the tournament begins March 26 and culminates with the Frozen Four in Las Vegas on April 9 and 11 .
